description: Company and project knowledge base contract for the digital-office agent system.

company.label: Company global knowledge base
company.purpose: Shared rules, facts, approved methodologies, reusable decisions, and reference material for all projects.

project.label: Project knowledge base
project.purpose: Project-specific documents, assets, decisions, meeting notes, generated reports, and working context.

licensed_industry_reference.label: Licensed industry reference layer
licensed_industry_reference.purpose: Provider-sold online industry knowledge mounted as controlled retrieval. It is not copied into company or project knowledge source storage.

agent_read_contract.conflict_policy: Report conflicts instead of silently overriding. Project source files win over company knowledge, licensed industry references, and KeyMemory relay memories for active project facts. Company-approved knowledge wins over licensed references when it expresses tenant-specific policy. System and company security rules still win over project rules unless the user explicitly approves a non-security exception.
agent_read_contract.citation_policy: Agents must cite source entry ids or filenames when using knowledge base facts.

customer_owned_external_kb.rule: Customer-owned sources may be synced or indexed according to admin settings and third-party permissions.

provider_sold_industry_kb.rule: Provider-sold knowledge is mounted as controlled retrieval only. Do not store readable source documents in company or project knowledge bases.

hard_rules: Never promote a project methodology summary directly into the company knowledge base without a draft report.
The GUI must allow the user to edit the draft before approval.
Only approved drafts may be copied to knowledge/company/methodologies/approved.

do_not_use_for: active project facts that conflict with source files
plain text API keys in ordinary memory
keymemory_policy.authority_rule: KeyMemory can tell an Agent where the project left off, but not override current project source documents, user-uploaded evidence, or approved project decisions.
